name: opensearch-mcp
instructions: Use this backend for indexed evidence search, aggregation, timeline analysis, ingest, and enrichment. It is not an independent reference source; use reference-providing backends for grounding.

data_plane.notes: Derived, rebuildable search plane. Indexed documents are case-scoped (case-<case_id>-<type>-<host>) and carry vhir.case_id / vhir.evidence_id / vhir.provenance_id stamped by the ingest job adapter. Index and provenance metadata are registered in authoritative Postgres (app.opensearch_indices / app.opensearch_ingest_provenance). In DB-active mode, ingest/enrich status is served from durable Postgres job state (app.job_status_public / app.opensearch_ingest_status) and host-identity decisions/corrections are recorded in app.host_identity_decisions; the local ingest-status JSON and host-dictionary.yaml are parser-compatibility/debug artifacts only and cannot change DB authority. Hostname detection/index naming remains required derived parser metadata. Holds no authority over cases, evidence seal, findings, or reports.

tools.name: opensearch_search
tools.description: Search indexed case evidence with OpenSearch query_string syntax. Supports time_from/time_to bounds, sort (field:asc|desc), and offset pagination, and compact projection. Use for targeted lookups by indicator, user, IP, hash, process, or field value.
tools.when_to_use: Use for targeted searches across already indexed case evidence (by indicator, user, IP, hash, process, or field value), with optional time bounds and pagination.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid before evidence has been ingested, for frequency counts (use opensearch_aggregate), for activity spikes (use opensearch_timeline), for exact totals (use opensearch_count), for one full document (use opensearch_get_event), or when you need independent reference grounding.
tools.output_notes: Returns indexed case records; use tools/list for query syntax and schema details.
tools.output_shape: SearchOut: total, total_capped (true => total is a gte lower bound; call opensearch_count for exact), returned, offset, compact, results[] of {id, index, fields, truncated[]}, advisories[].
tools.response_shaping: compact=True (default) drops bloat fields and truncates values to 500 chars; page with limit/offset rather than widening the query; expand a single hit with opensearch_get_event.

usage_examples.description: Minimal: find a process by name
Scoped + time-bounded
Full document view, second page, oldest-first

tools.defer_loading_rationale: Core analysis verb used in nearly every session; keep always-loaded.

tools.name: opensearch_count
tools.description: Return an exact document count matching a query_string filter, with no documents.
tools.when_to_use: Use to size a search result set or verify index population before retrieving records.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid when you need per-value counts (use opensearch_aggregate) or the documents themselves (use opensearch_search).
tools.output_notes: Returns counts only; follow with search or aggregate for evidence details.
tools.output_shape: CountOut: count (exact document count for the query in scope).
tools.response_shaping: Cheapest possible probe — one integer, no docs.

usage_examples.description: Count all docs in scope
Count successful logons

tools.defer_loading_rationale: Low-frequency helper; opensearch_search already reports total. Tool-Search defer_loading candidate.

tools.name: opensearch_aggregate
tools.description: Group indexed evidence by a field (terms aggregation, top-N by count) with an optional query_string pre-filter. For frequency analysis of event codes, users, hosts, processes, or IPs.
tools.when_to_use: Use for scoping distributions such as event codes, users, hosts, processes, or IPs.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid when you want individual documents (use opensearch_search) or a simple distinct value list without ranking (use opensearch_field_values).
tools.output_notes: Aggregation output identifies pivots but is not itself a finding.
tools.output_shape: AggregateOut: field, total_docs (matched before bucketing), buckets[] of {key, count}, truncated (true => more buckets than limit exist).
tools.response_shaping: Returns only the requested buckets, never raw docs. CSV/registry text fields need '.keyword'; evtx fields like event.code are already keyword.

usage_examples.description: Top event codes in the active case
Top file paths (text field needs .keyword), filtered

tools.defer_loading_rationale: Frequent pivot tool; keep always-loaded alongside search/timeline.

tools.name: opensearch_get_event
tools.description: Retrieve one complete indexed document by its _id and exact case-* index, with every field and no truncation.
tools.when_to_use: Use when a search or timeline result gives a specific document ID to inspect in full.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for many documents (page opensearch_search instead); this is a single-document lookup and the index must be exact, not a wildcard.
tools.output_notes: Returns one indexed event/document for evidence review.
tools.output_shape: GetEventOut: id, index, fields (full, untruncated), truncated[] (empty), note.
tools.response_shaping: The deliberate counterpart to compact search: pay the full-document cost for exactly one record, identified from a prior search hit.

usage_examples.description: Expand a specific search hit

tools.defer_loading_rationale: Used only to expand a known search hit; Tool-Search defer_loading candidate.

tools.name: opensearch_timeline
tools.description: Build a date histogram of event counts over time (interval Ns/Nm/Nh/Nd) to find activity bursts. Supports time_from/time_to bounds and a query_string filter.
tools.when_to_use: Use to find activity spikes and temporal windows, then scope opensearch_search with time_from/time_to.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for value distributions (use opensearch_aggregate) or for the events themselves (use opensearch_search).
tools.output_notes: Timeline buckets are scoping aids; inspect underlying events before conclusions.
tools.output_shape: TimelineOut: total_docs, interval, buckets[] of {time (ISO-8601), count} (sparse — empty buckets omitted), advisories[].
tools.response_shaping: Returns bucket counts only. Buckets are warned at the configured ceiling and never silently truncated; narrow with time_from/time_to or widen the interval.

usage_examples.description: Hourly histogram of process creations
15-minute resolution over a known window

tools.name: opensearch_field_values
tools.description: List unique values for a field (terms aggregation) with counts and an optional query_string filter, for value discovery before writing narrower searches.
tools.when_to_use: Use to discover field values (e.g. usernames, process names) before writing narrower searches.
tools.avoid_when: Use opensearch_aggregate instead when ranking/top-N matters; that path is the same terms aggregation framed for frequency.
tools.output_notes: Returns value inventory for indexed evidence fields.
tools.output_shape: FieldValuesOut: field, values[] of {value, count}, truncated (true => more distinct values exist than returned).
tools.response_shaping: Returns the value inventory only. CSV/text fields need '.keyword'; raise limit (hard cap 500) to widen.

usage_examples.description: Distinct event-log providers present
Distinct users, filtered to a host

tools.defer_loading_rationale: Overlaps opensearch_aggregate; lower-frequency discovery helper and a Tool-Search defer_loading candidate.

tools.name: opensearch_status
tools.description: Backend health/readiness check: OpenSearch cluster connectivity plus per-case index document counts. Deprecated tool form; prefer the opensearch://cluster/status resource.
tools.when_to_use: Use as the backend health/readiness check and to confirm per-case index state.
tools.avoid_when: Deprecated tool form — prefer the resource opensearch://cluster/status. For one case's artifact/coverage breakdown use opensearch_case_summary.
tools.output_notes: Health/status output only; it is not case evidence.
tools.output_shape: StatusOut: cluster_status (single-node yellow may be annotated normal), indices[] of {index, docs, size, status}, total_indices.
tools.response_shaping: Compact cluster + index roll-up; also exposed as a read-only resource to keep it out of the tool budget.

usage_examples.description: Cluster + per-case index health

tools.defer_loading_rationale: Deprecated tool form mirrored by a resource; Tool-Search defer_loading candidate pending removal at/after D27b.

tools.name: opensearch_shard_status
tools.description: Report shard usage and capacity headroom across the cluster. Deprecated tool form; prefer the opensearch://cluster/shards resource.
tools.when_to_use: Use for operational readiness before large ingest or when indexing fails.
tools.avoid_when: Deprecated tool form — prefer the resource opensearch://cluster/shards. Administrative signal, not investigation evidence.
tools.output_notes: Administrative capacity signal; not investigation evidence.
tools.output_shape: ShardStatusOut: current_shards, max_shards_per_node, data_nodes, max_total, headroom_pct, status (ok>=10% | warning>=2% | critical<2%), top_indices_by_shard_count[].
tools.response_shaping: Capacity roll-up with only the top shard-heavy indices; also exposed as a read-only resource.

usage_examples.description: Check shard headroom before a large image ingest

tools.defer_loading_rationale: Deprecated, operations-only; mirrored by a resource. Tool-Search defer_loading candidate pending removal at/after D27b.

tools.name: opensearch_case_summary
tools.description: Complete coverage overview of indexed evidence for a case — the first call in any indexed investigation. Returns hosts, artifact families, per-family doc counts, time range, enrichment state, and coverage gaps with exact fill commands.
tools.when_to_use: Use after ingest or at session start to understand indexed coverage and gaps.
tools.avoid_when: Skip only when you already hold a current summary this session; for a single artifact's frequency use opensearch_aggregate.
tools.output_notes: Coverage gaps guide next actions; verify important claims with underlying records.
tools.output_shape: CaseSummaryOut: case_id, hosts[], artifacts{family:{docs,hosts[],indices[]}}, total_docs, time_range{earliest,latest}, enrichment{...}, coverage_state{disk_artifacts,memory,enrichment,gaps[]}, investigation_hints[], warnings[].
tools.response_shaping: include_fields defaults False to keep the summary compact; the per-artifact field/type map is the bulk of the payload — request it only when picking aggregation fields.

usage_examples.description: Coverage map for the active case
Coverage map for a named case
Include field/type maps to plan aggregations

tools.defer_loading_rationale: High-frequency entry point; keep always-loaded so the agent can orient at session start without a Tool-Search round trip.

tools.name: opensearch_inspect_container
tools.description: Survey a forensic container (E01/raw/zip) without mounting it: integrity, size, partitions, and acquisition metadata. Run during evidence survey before deciding what to ingest.
tools.when_to_use: Use during evidence survey before deciding what to ingest.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid after ingest planning is done; follow with opensearch_ingest(dry_run=True) for the full plan.
tools.output_notes: Preview output helps plan ingest and does not modify evidence.
tools.output_shape: InspectContainerOut: path, resolved_path, container_type (e01|raw|file|unknown), tool_available, size_bytes, size_human, hashes{}, partitions[], acquiry_info{}, raw_info (truncated).
tools.response_shaping: raw_info is truncated fdisk/img_stat text — a preview, not the full tool dump. Read-only; never modifies evidence.

usage_examples.description: Preview an E01 before ingest

tools.defer_loading_rationale: SURVEY-phase tool used once per evidence item; Tool-Search defer_loading candidate.

tools.name: opensearch_ingest
tools.description: Discover and ingest forensic artifacts into OpenSearch. dry_run=True by default — the agent must explicitly set dry_run=False to write. Supports container/artifact-dir auto-detect, format override, include/exclude artifact filters, memory tiers/plugins, and VSS.
tools.when_to_use: Use after examiner approval to index evidence for structured search.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid setting dry_run=False until the target evidence and ingest plan are clear; use force=True only for an intentional re-ingest when the case already has docs.
tools.output_notes: Mutating operation scoped to the OpenSearch index; dry runs should precede writes.
tools.output_shape: IngestOut: status (preview|started|containers_detected|multi_started|already_indexed|failed|queued), case_id, job_id, job_type, dispatched_to, next_step, plan{}, container{}, already_indexed{}, suggested_hostname, warning, pid, run_id, log_file, note, details{}. Disk/E01 ingest returns status=queued + job_id (dispatched to sift-opensearch-worker@); poll job_status(job_id).
tools.response_shaping: Returns a plan/run reference (run_id, log_file) rather than streaming ingest output — poll opensearch_ingest_status for progress. The 'password' arg is a SECRET, redacted from audit/logs/results.

usage_examples.description: Preview an auto-detected container (default dry run)
Ingest a specific delimited artifact set for one host
Deep memory analysis, write

tools.name: opensearch_ingest_status
tools.description: Check running/recent ingest and enrichment operations for a case ("*" for all cases), with the per-host artifact checklist. Also the status channel for async enrichment.
tools.when_to_use: Use to monitor ingest progress or troubleshoot recent ingest runs (~every 30s while active); poll async enrichment with artifact_name 'intel'/'triage'.
tools.avoid_when: Operational status only — inspect indexed records (opensearch_search) for evidence, not this.
tools.output_notes: Operational status only; inspect indexed records for evidence.
tools.output_shape: IngestStatusOut: ingests[] of {case_id, status, pid, elapsed, total_indexed, bulk_failed, hosts_complete/total, artifacts_complete/total, log_file, checklist[], message, halt_reason, errors[], next_steps[], warnings[], details{}}, message.
tools.response_shaping: Returns a structured progress summary plus a log_file reference, not the raw run log.

usage_examples.description: Poll the active case's runs
Show runs across all cases

tools.defer_loading_rationale: Used only while an ingest/enrich run is in flight; Tool-Search defer_loading candidate.

tools.name: opensearch_enrich_intel
tools.description: Enrich indexed evidence with OpenCTI threat intel. Async — returns immediately with a run_id. Requires enrichment:intel scope. Status is pollable via opensearch_ingest_status (artifact_name='intel').
tools.when_to_use: Use after indexed indicators exist and CTI context would help prioritize pivots.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid when OpenCTI is unavailable or when indicators are speculative. Use dry_run=True first to size the IOC corpus.
tools.output_notes: Starts asynchronous enrichment; follow status/results before relying on output. No OpenCTI credentials or OpenSearch passwords are returned. Enrichment cannot approve findings, alter evidence, or decide reports.
tools.output_shape: EnrichIntelOut: status (preview|started), case_id, ips, hashes, domains, total_iocs (preview counts), pid, run_id, log_file, note. Async on write — poll opensearch_ingest_status (artifact_name=='intel').
tools.response_shaping: Returns counts + a run reference, never IOC dumps or OpenCTI/OpenSearch credentials.

usage_examples.description: Size the IOC corpus without lookup
Launch async enrichment for a case

tools.defer_loading_rationale: CORRELATE-phase, scope-gated, add-on-dependent (OpenCTI); Tool-Search defer_loading candidate.
tools.secret_leak_guarantee: No OpenCTI credentials, OpenSearch passwords, DB DSNs, or service tokens are returned in any response.

tools.name: opensearch_list_detections
tools.description: List Security Analytics (Sigma) detection findings for triage pivots, filtered by severity/detector_type; falls back to a Hayabusa query suggestion when the Sigma plugin is unavailable or empty.
tools.when_to_use: Use to identify indexed Sigma/Security Analytics detections for triage pivots.
tools.avoid_when: Detection hits are leads, not conclusions — validate against source events and surrounding context before recording a finding.
tools.output_notes: Detection hits require validation against source events and surrounding context.
tools.output_shape: ListDetectionsOut: findings[] of {id, timestamp, index, rules[] of {name, tags[]}, matched_docs}, total, returned, offset, suggestion (Hayabusa fallback when present).
tools.response_shaping: Paginate with limit/offset; the per-finding payload references matched docs by count, not by embedding them.

usage_examples.description: All detections, first page
High-severity detections only

tools.defer_loading_rationale: Depends on the optional Security Analytics plugin; lower-frequency. Tool-Search defer_loading candidate.

tools.name: opensearch_fix_host_mapping
tools.description: Correct a wrong host.id mapping across all indexed documents (reindexes affected docs). Canonical tool; the deprecated alias opensearch_host_fix is preserved by the tool registry for one cutover cycle. Scoped to OpenSearch index/alias and host-identity derived state. In DB-active mode the correction is recorded as an authoritative Postgres host-identity receipt (source/canonical/actor/tool/affected index IDs/audit id); the local host-dictionary.yaml is a parser-compatibility artifact only. In DB-active mode WITHOUT a recorder, the correction is DENIED with typed guidance (fail closed).
tools.when_to_use: Use only when indexed host identity is known to be wrong and needs correction.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid before confirming the canonical host mapping with the examiner or evidence. In DB-active mode WITHOUT a receipt recorder the correction is DENIED (fail closed).
tools.output_notes: Mutates indexed OpenSearch documents and host-identity derived metadata only; it does not alter original evidence, cases, findings, or reports. Returns no local filesystem paths in DB-active mode. DB-active mode requires receipt recorder; absence causes typed denial.
tools.output_shape: FixHostMappingOut: status ('complete'), raw, new_canonical, docs_updated, host_identity_authority, host_identity_decision_id (DB receipt), audit_id, dict_path/dict_saved (legacy non-DB mode only), details{}.
tools.response_shaping: Returns the correction receipt (decision/audit ids, docs_updated), not the reindexed documents.

usage_examples.description: Re-map a mis-attributed host across the case

tools.defer_loading_rationale: Rare, sensitive admin correction; Tool-Search defer_loading candidate.
